texto: We tell stories to talk out the trouble in our lives, trouble otherwise so often so unspeakable. It is one of our main ways of making our lives sensible. Trying to live without stories can make us crazy. They help us recognize what we believe to be most valuable in the world, and help us identify what we hold demonic.
explicacao: Citação de William Kittredge sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “We tell stories to talk out the trouble in our lives, trouble otherwise so often so unspeakable. …” articula reflexao, stories, trouble em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

Trying to live without stories can make us crazy. They help us recognize what we believe to be most valuable in the world, and help us identify what we hold demonic.

texto: How do you go to your own house when something has gone bad on the inside, when it doesn't seem like your place to live anymore, when you almost cannot recall living there although it was the place you mostly ate and slept for all your grown-up life? Try to remember two or three things about living there. Try to remember cooking one meal.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a William Kittredge: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “How do you go to your own house when something has gone bad on the inside, when it doesn't seem l…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, home, loss pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e loss.

texto: Sometimes there is no choice but to walk into your own house. Far away, you think, and you do not want to see. You come home and you say do not tell me. You say, I have hunted the elk all over the snowfields of the Selway, and I do not want to know what happened here. And then there is a morning you walk in and take a look in your own house, like any traveler.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a William Kittredge: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Sometimes there is no choice but to walk into your own house. Far away, you think, and you do not…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, change, home pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bom-dia e mudanca.

texto: Don't worry about meaning. If a story's any good, it can't help but have meaning. Let the PhDs tell you what your story means.

texto: We must define a story which encourages us to make use of the place where we live without killing it, and we must understand that the living world cannot be replicated.
explicacao: Orientação direta de William Kittredge sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “We must define a story which encourages us to make use of the place where we live without killing…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a vida, memoir, reflexao, útil em momentos de reflexao.
